what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 inline SVG icon showing two connected house-like outlines, using stroked paths with no fill.
observations: Uses a 24x24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
Draws the icon with four path elements.
Uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor" so color inherits from text or CSS context.
Sets stroke width to 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The left/front house has a roof, body, and small doorway; the right/rear house extends behind it.
standouts: Represents multiple houses in a compact single icon.
Uses only path strokes and inherited color.
conventions: Follows common line-icon SVG conventions: currentColor stroke, no fill, rounded caps and joins.
Uses absolute path commands in compact inline markup.
Does not include title, desc, role, or aria attributes inside the SVG.
concerns: No embedded accessible label or description is present in the file itself.
notes: A reusable line icon for a multi-house or property-group concept.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component or embedded in the SVG asset?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a multi-house icon, and the SVG content renders two house outlines consistent with that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a themeable, lightweight icon for representing more than one house or dwelling.
novelty: The composition layers a smaller front house with a taller rear/right house while staying within a simple line-icon vocabulary.
contribution: Contributes a reusable multi-dwelling icon shape that a fused system would keep as a housing or property-group visual substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This is a compact currentColor SVG line icon depicting two house outlines in a 24x24 canvas.
intention_for_embedding: This asset was for representing multiple houses, dwellings, or grouped properties in a design system icon set.
